title: Install CakePHP application on server with the traditional way on Ubuntu 22.0 server

excerpt: In this article, I want to make a Monolithic Server to run the Cakephp application. I know that Microservices and central systems are…

content.text: Install CakePHP application on server with the traditional way on Ubuntu 22.0 server

content.html: In this article, I want to make a Monolithic Server to run the Cakephp application. I know that Microservices and central systems are ubiquitous today. Still, this company has only one developer, and they don’t have any DevOps team, so I prefer to use the VPS server as a Monolithic server.

content.alt: Install CakePHP application on server with the traditional way on Ubuntu 22.0 server

content.html: I started my configuration based on the below article and then changed my design based on business needs.
Absolutely, we need Nginx to handle large amounts of TPS. on my expression Nginx is hardly robust and can handle a million requests per second and serve server without any problem.
Honestly, Mysql or MariaDB is perfect for this project. They maximum has 10K records each year, so they don’t have a severe problem with Mysql.
How do I handle the below bug whenever I want to run Mysql?
MySQL Failed! Error: SET PASSWORD has no significance for user ‘root’@’localhost’ as the authentication method used doesn’t store authentication data in the MySQL server.

content.code: ALTER USER 'root'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED WITH mysql_native_password by 'mynewpassword';

content.html: I use Mysql Extention in Vscode because I want to connect directly via the ssh tunnel to the Mysql tables.
In another way, we can use ssh on the server and write the direct command in the bash console.
For loading SQL files into the database, easily upload SQL files via FileZilla and then load with the below command.
If you want to delete the database using the below command. Be careful to use this command; the database will be terminated after this command.
If you use “Group by” in your queries, you will need this command to activate Group by for the query.

content.text: Make sure Mysql DB is secure.

content.html: Security is an essential part of all Databases, According to the following command, Mysql will disconnect the anonymous connection and force you to set up a strong password for the DB.
PHP 7.4 is so old, and it was deprecated but but my old code only runs on PHP 7.4 :((
The way of installing PHP 7.4 on the server has been written below. You don’t need to tell me PHP is deprecated and this application was about five years ago.

content.code:
sudo
apt-get update
sudo
apt -y install software-properties-common
sudo add-apt-repository ppa:ondrej/php
sudo
apt-get update
sudo
apt install php7.4-fpm php-mysql

content.html: For running Nginx, it is necessary to make a file and use GIT to pull from the server. As I mentioned, we don’t have a DevOps team to run a pipeline for this problem.

content.code:
sudo
mkdir /var/www/your_domain
sudo
chown -R $USER:$USER /var/www/your_domain
sudo
chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/your_domain
sudo vim /etc/nginx/sites-available/your_domain

content.html: Instead of using a CI/CD pipeline to deploy the system, we use a traditional way to pull code from the Main branch into the Monolotic server. This method has many disadvantages, but it’s a good solution whenever I have only one developer.

content.text: git add user name and email

content.html: Activate your configuration by linking to the configuration file from Nginx’s sites-enabled directory:
Then, unlink the default configuration file from the /sites-enabled/ directory:
This will tell Nginx to use the configuration next time it is reloaded. You can test your configuration for syntax errors by running the following:

content.code:
nginx: the configuration file /etc/nginx/nginx.conf syntax is oknginx: configuration file
/etc/nginx/nginx.conf test is successful

content.html: Your new website is now active, but the web root /var/www/your_domain is still empty. Create an index.html file in that location so that you can test that your new server block works as expected:
https://computingforgeeks.com/install-cakephp-framework-on-ubuntu/
copy app.php in config file
Make tmp directory , and cache and grant full permission to them.
Makeing API in Cakephp

content.code: bin/cake bake all productdetails  --prefix adminbin/cake bake all banners  --prefix admin

content.text: Transfer Data from one server to another server

content.code:
scp -P 44 path/to/local/file.ext user@remote-host:path/to/remote/file.extscp -r source
user@target:destscp -P port -r  ./uploads root@ip:/var/www/uploads

content.html: change all files permission to 644 and 755

content.code:
find  ./path  -type f -exec chmod 644 '{}' \;sudo find ./path -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \;sudo
chmod 777 -R tmpsudo chmod 777 -R logs

content.html: base on below articles I made cronjob for backup system then I copy via scp to another server.

content.text: Secure Nginx with Let’s Encrypt on Ubuntu 22.04

content.html: https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/how-to-secure-nginx-with-let-s-encrypt-on-ubuntu-22-04
https://community.cloudflare.com/t/redirect-example-com-to-www-example-com/78348

content.code:
root .htaccess: <IfModule mod_rewrite.c>   RewriteEngine on   RewriteRule ^(\.well-known/.*)$ $1 [L]
RewriteRule    ^$ app/webroot/    [L]   RewriteRule    (.*) app/webroot/$1 [L]   AddDefaultCharset
UTF-8 </IfModule>app/.htaccess:<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>    RewriteEngine on    RewriteRule
^(\.well-known/.*)$ $1 [L]    RewriteRule    ^$    webroot/    [L]    RewriteRule    (.*)
webroot/$1    [L]    AddDefaultCharset UTF-8  </IfModule>
